description: Stable llmparty DAG-mode agent tool definitions. This file is the cross-language source of truth for tool names, descriptions, input schemas, and output schemas. All DAG agents may register the same tools; llmparty authorizes each call server-side from the current session, execution profile, task, WorkItem, and WorkItemRun context.

tools.name: getContext
tools.title: Get DAG work context
tools.description: Fetch the current DAG-managed context for this agent session. Planning agents receive task, DAG, signals, workspace summary, and available execution profile context. Execution agents receive the current WorkItemRun, inputs, dependencies, expected outputs, and acceptance criteria. The caller must not provide task, WorkItem, run, or profile identity; llmparty derives it from the current session.

mode.description: Authorized DAG agent mode for the current session.

dag.description: Current approved or draft DAG context when relevant.

work_item.description: Current WorkItem context for execution agents.

work_item_run.description: Current WorkItemRun context for execution agents.

signals.description: Relevant signals for planning or execution.

execution_profiles.description: Relevant available execution profiles for planning agents.

tools.name: submitPlan
tools.title: Submit DAG plan
tools.description: Submit the planning agent's main output: an initial WorkItem DAG proposal or a DAG patch proposal. This tool proposes structured plan changes only; it does not approve, apply, or schedule the DAG. llmparty validates and authorizes the proposal server-side based on the current planning session and execution profile.

mode.description: Whether this submission proposes the initial DAG or a patch to an existing DAG.

summary.description: Human-readable summary of the proposed plan or patch.

dag.description: Initial DAG proposal when mode is initial_dag. Temporary WorkItem IDs are allowed; llmparty assigns authoritative IDs when applying an approved DAG.

patch.description: DAG patch proposal when mode is patch. Supported operations are validated by llmparty before approval or application.

assumptions.description: Assumptions used while producing this plan.

risks.description: Known risks in the proposed plan or patch.

accepted.description: Whether llmparty accepted the proposal for validation/approval workflow.

proposal_id.description: Identifier of the stored DAG proposal or patch proposal.

tools.name: submitResult
tools.title: Submit WorkItem result
tools.description: Submit the execution agent's main output for the current WorkItemRun. Implementation, validation, review, documentation, debugging, and similar WorkItems all use this same tool. This tool reports the current node result only; llmparty decides scheduling, retry, session reuse, and downstream DAG state server-side.

outputs.description: Logical work products produced or identified by this run. These may reference runtime artifacts or workspace files, but do not directly mutate the DAG.

ref.description: Optional external reference such as artifact:<id>, file:<path>, event:<id>, or URL.

failure.description: Failure or blocking detail when status is failed, blocked, or needs_input.

accepted.description: Whether llmparty accepted the result for the current WorkItemRun.

tools.name: raiseSignal
tools.title: Raise DAG signal
tools.description: Report a signal from the current agent session: missing input, replan request, risk, missing dependency, scope change, assistance need, or review request. Signals do not directly modify the DAG; llmparty records them and the Scheduler/RePlanner/Human approval flow decides what happens next.

related_refs.description: Optional references related to this signal, such as work_item:<id>, artifact:<id>, file:<path>, event:<id>, or signal:<id>.

accepted.description: Whether llmparty accepted and recorded the signal.
